matches.match_rationale: A unified internship database across 10-15 campuses requires staff and students to find relevant employer placements by meaning — matching student profiles, programs, and availability against employer descriptions without relying on exact keyword matches. Currently each campus manages its own informal lists, meaning duplicate outreach to the same employers and no shared discovery layer. Semantic search directly addresses the 'no unified internship database' component by enabling centralized cross-campus retrieval.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A central internship portal where students and coordinators search employer placements by program, role type, or location using natural language queries — surfacing relevant slots across all campuses from a single indexed repository.

matches.match_rationale: The core coordination problem is a classic matching/assignment problem: allocate students across campuses to employer slots subject to hard constraints (program fit, employer capacity, geographic proximity, supervision availability) and soft constraints (student preference, conversion potential, campus equity). No existing tool handles this cross-campus — coordinators manually negotiate, leading to students competing for the same slots. Constraint satisfaction directly solves the allocation logic.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-powered internship matching engine that ingests employer slots, student profiles, and campus-specific supervision capacity across all sites, then generates an optimal allocation plan that eliminates duplicate outreach and balances placements equitably across campuses.

matches.match_rationale: Internship coordination involves a repetitive multi-step workflow: employer outreach, slot confirmation, student matching, placement logistics, supervision assignment, and follow-up for conversion data. Currently each campus runs this end-to-end independently, duplicating effort 10-15x. A task automation agent can orchestrate this workflow centrally — handling employer communication, updating the shared database, routing placement confirmations, and triggering logistics steps — without requiring a human coordinator at each campus to manage each step.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A central internship workflow agent that manages the full placement lifecycle: sends standardized employer outreach, logs confirmed slots into the shared database, notifies relevant campus coordinators of matched students, and captures post-internship outcome data for conversion tracking.

matches.match_rationale: Employer information currently exists in fragmented forms: email threads, spreadsheets, campus-level documents, and informal notes. Building a unified employer vetting database requires extracting structured attributes — company name, industry, capacity, location, contact details, safety record indicators, and past placement history — from these unstructured sources. Entity extraction is the foundational step before any search, matching, or analytics capability can operate on the data.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An extraction pipeline that ingests legacy campus internship records (emails, spreadsheets, PDFs) and outputs a structured employer registry with normalized attributes, forming the data foundation for the unified internship platform.

matches.match_rationale: The pain point explicitly includes the absence of data on internship-to-permanent conversion rates. Once placement data is centralized, a propensity model can score employer-student pairs on likelihood of converting to permanent hire — enabling coordinators to prioritize high-conversion placements over volume. This directly addresses the strategic gap: operators cannot currently make data-driven decisions about which employer relationships to invest in.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A conversion propensity model trained on historical placement outcomes that scores new student-employer matches and surfaces the pairings most likely to result in permanent employment, helping coordinators prioritize placement effort across campuses.

matches.match_rationale: Each campus currently independently drafts employer outreach, internship agreements, and coordination communications, producing inconsistent quality and duplicating writing effort across 10-15 sites. AI text generation can standardize and accelerate the production of employer partnership letters, student placement confirmations, supervision briefings, and post-internship outcome request emails — reducing per-campus admin load while ensuring consistent brand and compliance language.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A template-driven generation system that produces campus-specific but centrally consistent employer outreach emails, placement confirmation letters, and supervision logistics briefs — eliminating the need for each campus coordinator to draft from scratch.
